houseStyleProcess: Build one connected mascot silhouette first: shadow, body, overlapping head, attached limbs, face, then details.
Every detail must be anchored to the surface it belongs to: spots/patches to bodyFront or face, ears/horns to the head, hooves to limbs/feet.
Never make core limbs/hooves/ears/spots read as floating stickers; attach, overlap, or embed them into the parent surface.
Use My Dude plush/toy proportions: rounded compact body, oversized friendly face, stubby limbs, glossy highlights.
For animal avatars, use mascotBody + mascotHead + stubbyArm/stubbyLeg + hoof/cuteEye/bodyPatch before decorative horns/ears/details.
For abstract shapes/forms/ideas/colors, still construct them as a mascot: readable core silhouette, face attached to core, details integrated into surfaces.
If a detail cannot be convincingly attached or embedded, omit it; omission is better than a floating sticker artifact.
Use large/medium/small shape hierarchy: one dominant readable silhouette, 2-4 medium identity cues, and only a few small details.
Run a mental silhouette test: if the avatar would not read as one connected character when filled black, simplify or omit details.
Use underlay/rim layers behind body/head when needed to make the connected silhouette visibly stronger.
Surface marks must be visibly inside or overlapping the parent body/head, not merely near it.
Face features must be proportional to the head: snouts/noses should be small secondary forms, eyes medium, and the talking mouth should remain readable but scaled from the face socket.
Limb details require a visible chain: if a hoof/paw/hand is present, render the corresponding arm/leg segment or omit the end detail.
Use one reusable mouth primitive for all mascot creations so lip-sync size/shape remains consistent across prompts while scaling by head/face size.
Animal mouths should use animal-appropriate materials/colors; cow mouths should read as warm brown/pink, not generic black UI holes.
Body spots/patches should sit inside the body mass and be visually centered enough to read as surface markings, not stray side objects.
Walking-ready mascots need legs long enough to visually connect hips to ground/feet; do not leave feet/hooves isolated below a body.
Render only one mouth per face: avoid drawing a separate smile line under an open talking mouth.
Mouth animation should support closed, mid, and open positions so consonants and syllable beats read more naturally than binary open/close.
Mascot mouths should stay intentionally tiny relative to the face; oversized mouths quickly read as pasted-on UI rather than character anatomy.
When browser speech does not expose reliable phonemes, approximate syllable attachment with very fast closed/mid/open cycling between word-boundary events.
Tiny mouths can still open vertically during speech; preserve small width while exaggerating height for readable talk motion.
For My Dude browser TTS, prefer very fast mouth pulses because word-boundary events are too sparse to feel syllable-attached.
Default My Dude-style mascots should match the original Buddy silhouette: oversized expressive head, compact body, and simple readable limbs.
For arbitrary generated creatures/objects, preserve a large-head-to-small-body ratio unless the prompt explicitly asks for realistic proportions.

attachmentMath.description: Client can place parts on proportional mascot rig sockets instead of absolute coordinates.
attachmentMath.rule: Prefer attach.socket for mascot pieces; use x/y only as small local offsets. End details like hooves/paws must attach to visible limb sockets and should be paired with visible arm/leg segments.
